title: chenopodium album var. missouriense
intro: Chenopodium album var. missouriense, also known as lamb's quarters, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Chenopodiaceae family, commonly found in North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of lamb's quarters include reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as supporting the immune system and digestive health. Herbal preparations of lamb's quarters can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
